head.subject: feat: P12 실제 파일럿 증빙 완성 and compact MCP transport 제공 (#46)

issues.message: Linked project metadata is ready for an operator-controlled preview start.
The target repository path, remote, branch, and working tree are ready for scope review.

nextAction.summary: Review the proposed files, scope, risks, and verification commands before implementation.

request.source:
{
  "kind": "design-ai-implementation-scope-request",
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "objective": "Add a verified before-and-after design iteration workflow that distinguishes resolved, persistent, introduced, and uncertain findings without upgrading missing evidence.",
  "intendedBehavior": [
    "Validate both canonical quality reports and require matching subject identity, brief, route, locale, and viewports before comparison.",
    "Count a removed finding as resolved only when its post-change lens passes; otherwise keep it uncertain.",
    "Preserve exact source references, SHA-256 digests, byte counts, lens transitions, finding decisions, approval boundaries, and remaining uncertainty.",
    "Expose one shared comparison contract through CLI, SDK, MCP, and Website Console, including compact MCP output for large exact-source pairs.",
    "Keep the operation read-only and make no adoption, production-quality, mutation, deployment, or external-write claim."
  ],
  "files": {
    "inspect": [
      "cli/lib/design-quality-contract.mjs",
      "cli/lib/review-comparison-contract.mjs",
      "cli/lib/review-comparison.mjs",
      "cli/lib/review-comparison.test.mjs",
      "cli/commands/review-compare.mjs",
      "cli/lib/mcp-server.mjs",
      "cli/lib/mcp-server.test.mjs",
      "cli/lib/capability-manifest.json",
      "cli/lib/capability-manifest.mjs",
      "cli/lib/capability-manifest.test.mjs",
      "cli/lib/dispatch.mjs",
      "cli/commands/help.mjs",
      "cli/sdk/index.mjs",
      "cli/sdk/index.d.ts",
      "cli/sdk/index.test.mjs",
      "cli/sdk/types.test.mjs",
      "cli/sdk/review-comparison-adapter.mjs",
      "cli/sdk/review-comparison-adapter.test.mjs",
      "docs/website-console/app.js",
      "docs/website-console/index.html",
      "docs/website-console/source-bundle.js",
      "docs/website-console/source-bundle.test.mjs",
      "docs/website-console/review-comparison.js",
      "tools/audit/capability_manifest.py",
      "tools/audit/package-contents.py",
      "tools/audit/package-smoke.py",
      "tools/audit/smoke_assertions.py",
      "README.md",
      "README.ko.md",
      "docs/REVIEW-COMPARISON.md",
      "docs/REVIEW-COMPARISON.ko.md",
      "docs/MCP-INTEGRATION.md",
      "docs/SDK.md",
      "docs/PRODUCT-READINESS.md",
      "docs/PRODUCT-SPECIALIZATION-PLAN.md",
      "docs/ROADMAP.md",
      "docs/SESSION-LOG.md",
      "docs/case-studies/verified-design-iteration.md",
      "CHANGELOG.md",
      "mkdocs.yml"
    ],
    "change": [
      "cli/lib/review-comparison-contract.mjs",
      "cli/lib/review-comparison.mjs",
      "cli/lib/review-comparison.test.mjs",
      "cli/commands/review-compare.mjs",
      "cli/lib/mcp-server.mjs",
      "cli/lib/mcp-server.test.mjs",
      "cli/lib/capability-manifest.json",
      "cli/lib/capability-manifest.mjs",
      "cli/lib/capability-manifest.test.mjs",
      "cli/lib/dispatch.mjs",
      "cli/commands/help.mjs",
      "cli/sdk/review-comparison-adapter.mjs",
      "cli/sdk/review-comparison-adapter.test.mjs",
      "cli/sdk/index.mjs",
      "cli/sdk/index.d.ts",
      "cli/sdk/index.test.mjs",
      "cli/sdk/types.test.mjs",
      "docs/website-console/review-comparison.js",
      "docs/website-console/app.js",
      "docs/website-console/index.html",
      "docs/website-console/source-bundle.test.mjs",
      "tools/audit/capability_manifest.py",
      "tools/audit/package-contents.py",
      "tools/audit/package-smoke.py",
      "tools/audit/smoke_assertions.py",
      "README.md",
      "README.ko.md",
      "docs/REVIEW-COMPARISON.md",
      "docs/REVIEW-COMPARISON.ko.md",
      "docs/MCP-INTEGRATION.md",
      "docs/SDK.md",
      "docs/PRODUCT-READINESS.md",
      "docs/PRODUCT-SPECIALIZATION-PLAN.md",
      "docs/ROADMAP.md",
      "docs/SESSION-LOG.md",
      "docs/case-studies/verified-design-iteration.md",
      "CHANGELOG.md",
      "mkdocs.yml"
    ],
    "generated": [
      "evidence/p13/**"
    ]
  },
  "dependencies": [],
  "migrations": [],
  "externalWrites": [
    {
      "system": "GitHub",
      "action": "push branch and open pull request",
      "destination": "sungjin9288/design-ai"
    }
  ],
  "verificationCommands": [
    "node --test cli/lib/review-comparison.test.mjs cli/sdk/review-comparison-adapter.test.mjs cli/lib/mcp-server.test.mjs docs/website-console/source-bundle.test.mjs",
    "npm run release:check"
  ],
  "risks": [
    "A missing post-change finding could be mislabeled as resolved even when the relevant lens was not actually verified.",
    "Reports from different subjects or review contexts could produce a misleading comparison unless identity and context match exactly.",
    "Large exact-source pairs could exceed the MCP output limit unless compact output is available from the first release.",
    "A comparison result could be mistaken for production quality or external adoption without an explicit claim boundary."
  ],
  "preExistingChanges": [],
  "release": {
    "commit": true,
    "push": true,
    "deployment": false
  }
}

value.objective: Add a verified before-and-after design iteration workflow that distinguishes resolved, persistent, introduced, and uncertain findings without upgrading missing evidence.
intendedBehavior: Validate both canonical quality reports and require matching subject identity, brief, route, locale, and viewports before comparison.
Count a removed finding as resolved only when its post-change lens passes; otherwise keep it uncertain.
Preserve exact source references, SHA-256 digests, byte counts, lens transitions, finding decisions, approval boundaries, and remaining uncertainty.
Expose one shared comparison contract through CLI, SDK, MCP, and Website Console, including compact MCP output for large exact-source pairs.
Keep the operation read-only and make no adoption, production-quality, mutation, deployment, or external-write claim.

externalWrites.action: push branch and open pull request

risks: A missing post-change finding could be mislabeled as resolved even when the relevant lens was not actually verified.
Reports from different subjects or review contexts could produce a misleading comparison unless identity and context match exactly.
Large exact-source pairs could exceed the MCP output limit unless compact output is available from the first release.
A comparison result could be mistaken for production quality or external adoption without an explicit claim boundary.

scope.objective: Add a verified before-and-after design iteration workflow that distinguishes resolved, persistent, introduced, and uncertain findings without upgrading missing evidence.
intendedBehavior: Validate both canonical quality reports and require matching subject identity, brief, route, locale, and viewports before comparison.
Count a removed finding as resolved only when its post-change lens passes; otherwise keep it uncertain.
Preserve exact source references, SHA-256 digests, byte counts, lens transitions, finding decisions, approval boundaries, and remaining uncertainty.
Expose one shared comparison contract through CLI, SDK, MCP, and Website Console, including compact MCP output for large exact-source pairs.
Keep the operation read-only and make no adoption, production-quality, mutation, deployment, or external-write claim.

externalWrites.action: push branch and open pull request

risks: A missing post-change finding could be mislabeled as resolved even when the relevant lens was not actually verified.
Reports from different subjects or review contexts could produce a misleading comparison unless identity and context match exactly.
Large exact-source pairs could exceed the MCP output limit unless compact output is available from the first release.
A comparison result could be mistaken for production quality or external adoption without an explicit claim boundary.

approvalGates.summary: Read only the approved file selectors during implementation.
Mutate only the approved change and generated-file selectors.
Respect the recorded ownership and handling of every pre-existing change.
Apply only the declared dependency changes; installation and network access remain separate.
Create or edit only declared migration files; running external-state migrations remains separate.
Write only the declared generated-file selectors.
External writes require a separate explicit approval and execution record.
Creating a commit requires a separate explicit gate.
Pushing changes requires a separate explicit gate.
Deployment requires a separate explicit gate.

issues.message: Repository identity, file selectors, verification, risks, and existing-change ownership are ready for human approval.

nextAction.summary: Review the immutable proposal and record a separate approval before reading or changing application source.

approver.name: project-owner
approver.reference: User authorized autonomous specialization development with verification, permission evidence, and grouped commits in the active Codex goal
